_scoring_convention.political_risk_score: 0–100 where 100 = zero political risk (higher is better)
_scoring_convention.operational_drag_score: 0–100 where 100 = maximum drag / worst efficiency (higher is worse)
_scoring_convention.rag_convention: GREEN = proceed, AMBER = proceed with conditions, RED = material concern

jurisdiction.name: Slovenia
jurisdiction.currency_eur_rate_date: N/A — EUR is the domestic currency since 1 January 2007
jurisdiction.currency_eur_rate_source: Slovenia adopted the Euro on 1 January 2007 (first former Yugoslav republic to join the Eurozone).
jurisdiction.data_staleness_warning: Slovenian RES policy is administered by the Agency for Energy (Agencija za energijo — AGEN-RS at agencija-energija.si) and the market operator Borzen d.o.o. (borzen.si). The TSO is ELES d.o.o. (eles.si). Verify current tender parameters at Borzen and current legislation at pisrs.si (Pravno-informacijski sistem — legal information system of the Republic of Slovenia).

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Energy Act (Energetski zakon — EZ-1) 2014, as amended; Energy Act-2 (EZ-2) proposed/enacted
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: Framework energy legislation; TSO (ELES) and DSO obligations; market framework; grid connection rights. NOTE: Slovenia was in the process of adopting an updated Energy Act (EZ-2) to implement revised EU energy directives — verify current primary legislation at pisrs.si.

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Act on Promotion of Renewable Energy Sources (Zakon o spodbujanju rabe obnovljivih virov energije — ZSROVE) 2021, as amended
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: Primary RES support legislation; competitive tender (dražba) mechanism; Borzen as market operator; AGEN-RS regulatory oversight

primary_energy_legislation.instrument: Resolution on the National Energy and Climate Programme (Resolucija o nacionalnem energetskem in podnebnem programu — ReNEPP) 2023–2030
primary_energy_legislation.relevance: RES target: 30–33% renewable energy by 2030; solar and wind as primary development technologies; nuclear (JEK 2 — new nuclear unit at Krško) under discussion

permitting_legislation.instrument: Spatial Management Act (Zakon o urejanju prostora — ZUreP-3) 2022
permitting_legislation.relevance: Spatial planning; municipal and national spatial plans; RES zone designation

permitting_legislation.instrument: Construction Act (Gradbeni zakon — GZ-1) 2017, as amended; GZ-1 (amended 2022)
permitting_legislation.relevance: Building permits (gradbeno dovoljenje); commissioning certificates; permitting procedural rules

permitting_legislation.instrument: Environmental Protection Act (Zakon o varstvu okolja — ZVO-2) 2022
permitting_legislation.relevance: EIA procedures; environmental permits; public participation

permitting_legislation.instrument: Nature Conservation Act (Zakon o ohranjanju narave — ZON) 1999, as amended
permitting_legislation.relevance: Natura 2000 (approximately 36% of Slovenian territory — significant constraint); protected species; EIA for projects in/near protected areas

permitting_legislation.instrument: Decree on RES development zones (Uredba o določitvi razvojnih območij za obnovljive vire energije) — 2023/2024
permitting_legislation.relevance: Slovenia has been designating 'RES development zones' where permitting is streamlined for solar and wind — a significant recent policy development; verify current zone map at AGEN-RS

tax_legislation.instrument: Corporate Income Tax Act (Zakon o davku od dohodkov pravnih oseb — ZDDPO-2) 2006, as amended
tax_legislation.relevance: 19% CIT rate. Investment relief (investicijska olajšava) for qualifying CapEx. No special RES-specific accelerated depreciation beyond general rules.

tax_legislation.instrument: Value Added Tax Act (Zakon o davku na dodano vrednost — ZDDV-1) 2006, as amended
tax_legislation.relevance: 22% standard VAT rate; input VAT recovery available

subsidy_control_legislation.relevance: EU framework for Slovenian RES support assessment

fdi_legislation.instrument: Act on Critical Infrastructure Protection (Zakon o kritični infrastrukturi) and relevant sector laws
fdi_legislation.relevance: Slovenia does not have a comprehensive standalone FDI screening law equivalent to Germany's AWG or Slovakia's 2022 Act. Some energy infrastructure protections apply through sector legislation and EU Regulation 2019/452.

permitting._notes: Slovenian permitting is undergoing positive reform. The designation of RES development zones (2023/2024) is intended to significantly streamline permitting for solar and wind projects within designated areas. Outside designated zones, the full EIA + spatial plan process applies. Natura 2000 coverage (~36% of territory) is a material constraint for highland/forest wind sites.

solar_utility.primary_authority: Agencija za okolje (ARSO — Slovenian Environment Agency) for EIA/AA; municipality for spatial plan and building permit; AGEN-RS for energy permit; ELES/DSO for grid connection
solar_utility.primary_process: Spatial plan compliance check → EIA screening (ARSO) → Full EIA if required → Building permit (gradbeno dovoljenje) → Energy production permit (AGEN-RS) → Grid connection (ELES/Elektro) → Commissioning
solar_utility.legal_basis: ZUreP-3; GZ-1; ZVO-2; ZSROVE; AGEN-RS regulations
solar_utility.res_zone_note: Projects within designated RES development zones benefit from streamlined permitting (reduced EIA requirements; pre-cleared spatial planning). Locate zone map at AGEN-RS (agencija-energija.si) or Ministry of Infrastructure.
solar_utility.solar_resource: Southern Slovenia (Primorska region, Kras plateau, Goriška Brda, Pannonian plain of Prekmurje) achieves 1,300–1,500 kWh/kWp p.a. — good resource by Central European standards; northern Slovenia (Gorenjska) lower.
solar_utility.notes: Slovenia is actively promoting utility solar. The pre-designated RES zone approach is a genuine positive for development velocity. Ground-mounted solar on Pannonian plain (Prekmurje, Pomurje) is the primary utility-scale opportunity.

onshore_wind.primary_authority: ARSO (EIA/AA); municipality + Ministry of Infrastructure for spatial plan; GZ-1 building permit; AGEN-RS energy permit; ELES grid connection
onshore_wind.primary_process: National/local spatial plan for wind energy areas → EIA/AA → Building permit → Energy permit → Grid connection → Commissioning
onshore_wind.wind_challenge: Slovenia has very limited onshore wind development (~6 MW installed) despite reasonable ridge-top resource. The combination of mountainous terrain (Dinaric Alps, Julian Alps — Alpine zone covers ~50% of territory), extensive Natura 2000 constraints, and historically slow spatial planning processes has restricted development. Coastal Karst region and Pannonian hills (Goričko, eastern Haloze) are the most viable wind areas. RES development zones and political momentum (post-2022 energy crisis) are creating new opportunities.
onshore_wind.rag_assessment: AMBER — limited but not blocked; RES zone designation is a genuine positive signal

offshore_wind.effective_status: Very limited — Slovenia has a short Adriatic coastline (46.6 km) in the Gulf of Trieste
offshore_wind.notes: Slovenia's coastline is too short and the Adriatic at that point too shallow and congested for meaningful offshore wind development. Offshore is not a relevant technology for Slovenia.

battery_storage_utility.primary_process: Building permit; ELES/DSO grid connection; AGEN-RS energy storage registration
battery_storage_utility.notes: BESS market developing — useful for solar smoothing in the Pannonian plain and supporting grid stability. ELES is integrating storage in grid planning.

transmission_operators.name: ELES d.o.o. (Elektro-Slovenija)
transmission_operators.region: Slovenian national TSO; state-owned; ENTSO-E member; operates 400kV/220kV/110kV transmission network

grid_connection.distribution_operators: Five regional DSOs: Elektro Celje d.d.; Elektro Gorenjska d.d.; Elektro Ljubljana d.d.; Elektro Maribor d.d.; Elektro Primorska d.d. — all regulated by AGEN-RS
grid_connection.legal_basis: EZ-1 (Energy Act) §§35–55; ELES Grid Code; AGEN-RS regulations; RES priority grid access
grid_connection.interconnections: Slovenia is well-interconnected: Italy (via 400kV lines through Okroglo/Krško), Austria (Tauern), Hungary, and Croatia. This central Alpine/Adriatic position is strategically advantageous for RES electricity export.

transmission_connected.notes: Growing solar pipeline in Prekmurje creating queue; ELES reinforcement investment underway

connection_cost_eur_per_mw.notes: Grid connection costs regulated by AGEN-RS; Prekmurje DSO (Elektro Maribor) facing growing queue from solar pipeline; reinforcement costs can be material for remote sites

support_mechanisms._rule_3a_note: Rule 3A: The Slovenian competitive tender premium (dražba) under ZSROVE is a ONE-WAY mechanism. Premium = max(0, reference price − market clearing price). When market price exceeds the reference price, the premium is zero — the generator retains full merchant upside with NO clawback.
support_mechanisms.primary_mechanism: Dražba (auction) — competitive tender premium; Borzen d.o.o. as market operator
support_mechanisms.legal_basis: ZSROVE (Act on Promotion of RES) 2021, as amended; AGEN-RS regulations; Borzen operating rules

mechanism_description.summary: Generator wins competitive Borzen auction and receives a reference premium. Sells electricity on HUPX/day-ahead market (Slovenia uses HUPX Hungarian Power Exchange reference for Slovenian bidding zone SI). Premium = max(0, reference price − HUPX-SI average). One-way: no clawback when market price exceeds reference.
mechanism_description.downside_treatment: PROTECTED — Borzen settlement body pays shortfall
mechanism_description.hupx_si_note: Slovenia's electricity market is integrated with HUPX (Hungarian Power Exchange) for market coupling purposes. The reference price is based on the SI bidding zone (HUPX-SI) price, which tracks Italian, Austrian, and Central European price signals.

auction.tender_frequency: Periodic rounds — Borzen publishes tender calls; verify at borzen.si

reference_price_eur_mwh_approx._staleness_warning: Reference prices change with each Borzen auction round. Verify at Borzen (borzen.si) and AGEN-RS before use.

solar_pv.notes: RES zone projects may have lower prices as grid and planning risk is reduced

tax._rule_3b_note: Rule 3B: 19% CIT. Investment relief (investicijska olajšava) for qualifying CapEx — 40% of qualifying investment deducted from taxable base (not from tax itself — a base deduction). Apply NPV analysis to timing benefit.

corporation_tax_structure.legal_basis: ZDDPO-2 §60 — 19% rate; verify any rate changes at pisrs.si

investment_relief.name: Investicijska olajšava (Investment Relief)
investment_relief.description: Companies investing in qualifying equipment, machinery, and intangibles (including RES assets) may deduct 40% of the qualifying investment amount from the taxable base (not from CIT directly). Available in the investment year; excess relief carried forward 5 years.
investment_relief._rule_3b_analysis: NPV of timing benefit: For a EUR 50m solar project with EUR 40m qualifying investment: 40% investment relief = EUR 16m deduction from taxable base. CIT saving: EUR 16m × 19% = EUR 3.04m (compared to no relief). Timing benefit: saving arises in investment year vs. accumulated depreciation over project life. NPV of EUR 3.04m CIT saving in year 1 vs. distributed over 15 years at 7% WACC: acceleration benefit ≈ EUR 1.2m. Moderate but material benefit.
investment_relief.note: Investment relief base deduction (40% of CapEx off taxable base) reduces taxable income — not a direct CIT credit. For a profitable company, effective benefit = 40% × qualifying investment × 19% CIT rate.

corporation_tax_structure.accelerated_depreciation_note: No special RES accelerated depreciation beyond standard rules. Solar PV assets: typically 12.5% declining balance (8-year effective life). ZDDPO-2 Annex sets depreciation rates.
corporation_tax_structure.dividend_wht_note: 0% dividend WHT for qualifying EU parent companies under EU Parent-Subsidiary Directive (ZDDPO-2 §71, implementing EU Directive 2011/96/EU)

vat.input_vat_recovery: Available for VAT-registered businesses; solar installations may have specific rules — verify with Slovenian tax adviser

ppa_market.legal_basis: Code of Obligations (Obligacijski zakonik — OZ) 2001; contractual freedom; no bespoke PPA statute
ppa_market.jurisdiction_enforcement: Slovenian courts (Vrhovno sodišče — Supreme Court; Višje sodišče za Slovenijo — High Court; Commercial Court Koper and Ljubljana). Proceedings typically 1–3 years for commercial disputes. Slovenian Chamber of Commerce and Industry Arbitration Court (ICC Ljubljana) available; ICC international arbitration.
ppa_market.market_liquidity: Developing — Slovenian manufacturing sector (automotive, pharmaceuticals, electronics) plus cross-border buyers from Italy and Austria
ppa_market.prominent_corporate_offtakers: Revoz d.d. (Renault subsidiary, Novo Mesto — automotive); KRKA d.d. (pharmaceuticals, Novo Mesto); Gorenje (Hisense subsidiary, appliances, Velenje); Petrol d.d. (energy company, Borzen market participant); Italian corporates via interconnection (Italy market integration)
ppa_market.italian_market_integration_note: Slovenia's interconnection with Italy (400kV lines) and its position in the Italian/Central European price zone creates access to Italian corporate PPA buyers (a much larger market). This cross-border PPA opportunity is a genuine differentiator for Slovenian projects.

guarantees_of_origin.regulator: Borzen d.o.o. — issues GoOs (garancije o izvoru) for Slovenian RES generators
guarantees_of_origin.note: Slovenian GoOs in EU AIB framework. Standard additional revenue stream.

credit_ratings.source_note: Slovenia maintains solid investment-grade ratings; sovereign performance has improved following 2012–2013 banking crisis resolution. Verify at agency websites.

sovereign_risk.political_stability: Centre-left government (Gibanje Svoboda, PM Golob since June 2022); stable coalition; strong pro-EU, pro-NATO orientation. Coalition politics typical of Slovenian parliamentary democracy (DZ).
sovereign_risk.energy_policy_direction: Current Golob government is significantly more RES-positive than predecessor (Janša government had slowed RES development). Strong push for solar deployment, wind zone designation, and green hydrogen. This is a material positive change in political environment for RES investors.

retroactive_policy_incidents.description: Slovenia has not retroactively modified contracted RES revenues. The regulatory framework has been generally stable for contracted projects.

banking_crisis_2012_2013.description: Slovenia experienced a banking sector crisis in 2012–2013 requiring state recapitalisation of major banks (Nova Ljubljanska banka — NLB, etc.). Avoided EU bailout but required significant state intervention. Banking sector has since stabilised; NLB successfully IPO'd (2018) and is a functioning commercial bank.
banking_crisis_2012_2013.investor_relevance: Banking crisis resolved — not a current risk. NLB privatisation was completed; banking sector now well-capitalised. Historical context only.

sovereign_risk.expropriation_risk: Very Low — EU/NATO member; constitutional property protections; ECHR
sovereign_risk.score_rationale: Deductions from 100: Banking crisis historical (-8), smaller economy (-5), limited natural RES resources (mountainous Alpine territory limits best-case wind/solar) (-5), coalition government transition risk (-2). Score 80 reflects solid governance with positive current policy trajectory.

grid_connection_drag.assessment: MEDIUM — ELES competent and professional; growing solar queue in Prekmurje; good interconnection with Italy/Austria for export routing; reinforcement investment underway

permitting_drag_solar_res_zone.assessment: LOW — within designated RES zones, streamlined process significantly reduces EIA burden; 18 months P50 is competitive

permitting_drag_solar_standard.assessment: MEDIUM — outside RES zones: full ZVO-2 EIA; spatial plan amendment; multiple authority involvement; Natura 2000 checks required

permitting_drag_onshore_wind.assessment: MEDIUM-HIGH — complex terrain; Natura 2000 in Alpine/Dinaric zones; long P50 (60 months); improving with RES zone designations but still challenging

bureaucratic_friction.assessment: MEDIUM — functional public administration; Slovenian language; ARSO (Environmental Agency) and Borzen are professional; some multi-authority coordination complexity; digital services growing

judicial_appeal_risk.assessment: MEDIUM — courts independent and functioning; proceedings 1–3 years; environmental challenge risk for wind projects in sensitive areas; ICC arbitration efficient alternative

operational_drag.primary_bottleneck: For solar: grid queue in Prekmurje; outside-RES-zone spatial plan process. For wind: Natura 2000 constraints and terrain; improving with RES zone designation.
operational_drag.res_zone_positive: The designation of pre-cleared RES development zones is a genuine and significant improvement in Slovenia's permitting environment. Projects within zones benefit from reduced EIA requirements and pre-cleared spatial planning. Verify current zone boundaries at AGEN-RS.
operational_drag.score_rationale: Score 56 reflects a mid-tier drag environment — improving due to RES zone policy, competent TSO, and positive political environment, offset by Natura 2000 constraints, growing solar grid queue, and some bureaucratic complexity.

fdi_restrictions.screening_regime: No comprehensive FDI screening law equivalent to Slovakia 2022 or Germany AWG; EU Regulation 2019/452 basic implementation; some sector-specific critical infrastructure protections
fdi_restrictions.practical_risk: Low — Slovenia is generally welcoming to EU/EEA investment; no material energy sector FDI restriction; participation in EU screening coordination mechanisms

tender_state_aid_status.status: Competitive tender mechanism designed for CEEAG compliance. Verify EC clearance at competition.ec.europa.eu.

retrospective_clawback_risk.assessment: Very Low — no retroactive modification history; ZSROVE 2021 designed under modern CEEAG standards; Golob government strongly pro-RES

financial_model_inputs._rule_3d_note: Rule 3D: Inputs for IRR, NPV, CapEx payback, DSCR, and LCOE modelling.
financial_model_inputs._rule_3c_note: Slovenia is EUR-denominated — no FX conversion required.
financial_model_inputs._rule_3b_note: 19% CIT. Investment relief: 40% of qualifying CapEx deducted from taxable base in investment year. NPV benefit: EUR 1.2m approx on EUR 50m project at 7% WACC (acceleration vs. distributed depreciation).

illustrative_solar_utility_prekmurje.investment_relief_note: 40% of qualifying CapEx deducted from taxable base in investment year. EUR 50m project: EUR 20m qualifying × 40% = EUR 8m base reduction → EUR 1.52m CIT saving in investment year vs. being distributed over depreciation life. NPV benefit: approx EUR 0.8–1.2m.

permitting_window_solar_res_zone.rationale: Within designated RES zones: 18 months P50; streamlined EIA; pre-cleared spatial planning; excellent policy momentum under Golob government; Pannonian plain (Prekmurje) has best irradiation

permitting_window_solar_standard.rationale: Outside RES zones: full ZVO-2 EIA + spatial plan process adds 30 months P50; multiple authorities; Natura 2000 checks required in some areas

permitting_window_onshore_wind.rationale: ~6 MW installed nationally but improving — RES zone designation for wind creating new opportunities; 60 months P50 is long but not a moratorium; Natura 2000 is primary constraint; Karst and Pannonian hills are viable

grid_connection.rationale: ELES competent; growing Prekmurje solar queue creates pressure; Italian interconnection is a strategic advantage for export; reinforcement investment underway

support_mechanism_availability.rationale: Borzen competitive tender operational and well-structured; one-way; Golob government strongly pro-RES; ZSROVE 2021 is a modern CEEAG-compliant framework

mechanism_type_risk.rationale: One-way; no clawback; no retroactive RES modification history; Golob government is the most RES-supportive Slovenian government in history

tax_efficiency.rationale: 19% CIT + 40% investment relief base deduction = effective reduction in early year tax base. 0% dividend WHT for EU parent companies. Competitive for mid-tier EU jurisdiction.

ppa_enforceability.rationale: Code of Obligations provides reliable enforcement; ICC/SCC arbitration available; Italian market integration creates access to larger PPA buyer universe; KRKA/Revoz as domestic counterparties

political_risk.rationale: A2/AA- sovereign; EU/NATO member; no retroactive RES incidents; current Golob government is strongly pro-RES — best political environment for RES investment in Slovenia's post-independence history

operational_drag.rationale: Score 56 — mid-tier but improving; RES zone designation is a genuine positive; ELES professional; Italian interconnection strategic advantage; trajectory is positive

fdi_restrictions.rationale: No comprehensive FDI screening law; welcoming environment for EU investment; no material energy sector restriction

eu_state_aid_risk.rationale: ZSROVE 2021 competitive tender CEEAG-compliant; no retroactive history; EC clearance process standard

rag_matrix.overall_rationale: Slovenia presents a genuinely attractive mid-tier RES investment opportunity with GREEN overall status. The combination of a strongly pro-RES Golob government, the ZSROVE 2021 competitive tender (one-way, no clawback), designated RES development zones with streamlined permitting, 19% CIT with 40% investment relief, 0% dividend WHT for EU parents, and access to Italian electricity markets via interconnection creates a balanced and improving investment environment. Primary constraints are: Prekmurje grid queue (growing solar pipeline), Natura 2000 constraints for wind (36% of territory), and a smaller total RES project market than Germany, Poland, or Spain. Suitable for Low Risk Core Infrastructure and Value-Add profiles; particularly attractive for investors with Italian market presence who can leverage the interconnection for PPA structuring.

comparison_table.1_permitting_window_scope: Solar within designated RES development zones (Pannonian plain — Prekmurje/Pomurje). Outside RES zones: 30 months P50. Onshore wind: 60 months P50.
comparison_table.3_primary_support_mechanism: Borzen competitive tender (dražba) under ZSROVE 2021; one-way premium; HUPX-SI market reference
comparison_table.4_effective_corporation_tax_rate_pct: 19% CIT; 0% dividend WHT for EU parent (Parent-Subsidiary Directive)
comparison_table.4_effective_tax_rate_note: 19% CIT. Investment relief: 40% of qualifying CapEx deducted from taxable BASE in investment year → timing benefit vs. standard depreciation. Effective combined benefit: EUR 1–1.5m NPV on EUR 50m project at 7% WACC.
comparison_table.4_key_incentive: Investment Relief (investicijska olajšava — ZDDPO-2 §55): 40% of qualifying CapEx deducted from taxable base in investment year (with 5-year carry-forward for excess); 0% dividend WHT for EU parent under Parent-Subsidiary Directive
comparison_table.5_ppa_legal_basis: Code of Obligations (OZ); ICC/SCC arbitration; Italian market integration via 400kV interconnection creates access to larger PPA buyer universe
comparison_table.8_revenue_floor_native: EUR 45–85/MWh approx. (Borzen auction reference — indicative; verify at Borzen and AGEN-RS)
comparison_table.8_revenue_floor_staleness_warning: Verify at Borzen (borzen.si) and AGEN-RS (agencija-energija.si). Reference prices change with each tender round. Staleness risk: HIGH.

disclaimer: This output is produced by Hyperion AI for indicative jurisdiction-screening purposes only. It does not constitute legal or financial advice. Users should obtain qualified local legal and financial counsel before committing capital. Hyperion AI complies with SRA Standards and Regulations (15/12/24 edition).

green_hydrogen.notes: Slovenia is not an IPCEI participant. National hydrogen strategy and action plan in development. EU cohesion and recovery funds available. Small market — hydrogen investment likely to be niche/industrial.

floating_solar.primary_support_mechanism: Tržna premija (market premium) under Zakon o spodbujanju rabe obnovljivih virov energije (ZSROVE), administered by Borzen.
floating_solar.notes: Permitting estimated as ground-mounted solar P50 plus 9 months for water authority consent. Irradiance comparable to ground-mounted at same latitude per JRC PVGIS. Verify national tender eligibility before use.

nuclear_smr.notes: Slovenia operates Krško NPP (50/50 with Croatia) which provides ~30% of Slovenian electricity. NEK 2 — a second large reactor at Krško — is under consideration as a Slovenia-led project. This is a large unit, not an SMR. No specific SMR programme. Source: IEA country review.

lng_gas.notes: No LNG terminal. Small Adriatic coastline. Receives gas via pipeline.

data_centre.notes: Insufficient published data for medium-confidence estimate.

natural_gas_lng.notes: Slovenia has gas-fired generation. EU Taxonomy Delegated Act (EU 2022/1214) transition eligibility applies for qualifying CCGT below 270gCO2e/kWh. No formal capacity market confirmed. CCGT CapEx estimate: EUR 900,000/MW (IEA mid-point reference).

biowaste_energy.notes: Insufficient published data for medium-confidence assessment. EU RED III compliance and feedstock sustainability criteria apply. Verify current Slovenian bioenergy support under ZSROVE framework before use.

coal_energy.notes: No major coal-fired power generation sector. EU Taxonomy-excluded. No coal support mechanism identified.

lh2_storage._overview: Liquid hydrogen (LH2) storage infrastructure: cryogenic storage at -253°C and associated liquefaction plant. Slovenia's NECP includes a hydrogen component and its electricity mix (~60–70% hydro) provides inherently low-carbon generation well-suited to green H2 electrolysis with a renewable PPA. The Italian interconnection (400kV) creates both export opportunities for LH2 and access to Italian corporate buyers. Safety regime: SEVESO III implemented via Uredba o preprečevanju in zmanjšanju posledic večjih nesreč (Uradni list RS, št. 22/16 in 68/19). Electricity cost for liquefaction is the primary OpEx risk.
lh2_storage.primary_support_mechanism: No dedicated LH2 storage support mechanism in Slovenia. Standalone LH2 facilities operate on a tolling or merchant basis. EU cohesion funds (ERDF, SRIP — Strategic Research and Innovation Partnership — H2 cluster via SPIRIT Slovenia) may provide CapEx grant support for qualifying H2 infrastructure. Investment relief (naložbena olajšava — ZDDPO-2 §55) provides 40% of qualifying CapEx deducted from taxable base. SPIRIT Slovenia (spiritslovenia.si) administers investment incentives. Source: SPIRIT Slovenia; ARSO; pisrs.si.
lh2_storage.staleness_warning: EU cohesion fund programme calls and SPIRIT Slovenia investment incentive terms are time-sensitive. Verify current availability at spiritslovenia.si and eu-skladi.si. Borzen RES tender parameters change per round — verify at borzen.si.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Uredba o preprečevanju in zmanjšanju posledic večjih nesreč (Uradni list RS, št. 22/16 in 68/19) — SEVESO III Regulation
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: Slovenian SEVESO III transposition. Lower tier (≥10 t flammable gas including LH2): notify ARSO, prepare MAPP and internal emergency plan. Upper tier (≥50 t flammable gas): detailed varnostno poročilo (safety report), external emergency plan (zunanji načrt zaščite in reševanja), public information duty, periodic ARSO inspection.

primary_safety_legislation.instrument: Zakon o varstvu pred požarom (ZVPoz) — Fire Protection Act (Uradni list RS, št. 3/07 — official consolidated text, as amended)
primary_safety_legislation.relevance: Fire safety classification and protection requirements for LH2 storage and dispensing; permits from Uprava RS za zaščito in reševanje (Administration for Civil Protection and Disaster Relief).
Environmental permit (okoljevarstveno dovoljenje) for LH2 facilities; administered by ARSO (Agencija RS za okolje — Slovenian Environment Agency); governs BOG atmospheric emissions.
Classification and handling of hydrogen as a dangerous chemical; REACH coordination.

planning_legislation.relevance: Building permits (gradbeno dovoljenje); commissioning certificates (uporabno dovoljenje); applies to LH2 facility construction.
Spatial plan compliance check; LH2 industrial facilities require compatible spatial designation (industrial zone).

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: Resolucija o nacionalnem energetskem in podnebnem programu (ReNEPP) 2023–2030
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: NECP includes H2 component; targets renewable electricity growth with hydrogen as storage and transport medium; identifies green H2 as priority decarbonisation tool.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: EU Hydrogen Bank — tender rounds (EC Regulation)
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: Slovenia is eligible as EU member state. LH2 production and enabling infrastructure may qualify for EU Hydrogen Bank auction support. Verify current round at innovation-fund.ec.europa.eu.

hydrogen_strategy_instruments.instrument: SRIP Energetika (Strategic Research and Innovation Partnership — Energy cluster)
hydrogen_strategy_instruments.relevance: Slovenia's H2 innovation cluster includes Kolektor Group (H2 components), GEN-I (H2 trading), and research institutions. SRIP programme may provide R&D co-funding.

seveso_iii_regime.competent_authority: ARSO (Agencija RS za okolje — Slovenian Environment Agency) jointly with IRSD (Inšpektorat RS za delo — Labour Inspectorate of the Republic of Slovenia)
seveso_iii_regime.lh2_classification: LH2 classified as flammable gas (Category 2 Annex I, Part 1 of SEVESO III / Uredba 22/16). Thresholds apply to total quantity on site.
seveso_iii_regime.lower_tier_obligations: Notify ARSO; prepare politika preprečevanja večjih nesreč (MAPP equivalent); internal emergency plan only.
seveso_iii_regime.upper_tier_obligations: Prepare detailed varnostno poročilo (safety report) acceptable to ARSO; zunanji načrt zaščite in reševanja (external emergency plan) prepared by regional civil protection authority; public information duty; periodic ARSO/IRSD inspection.
seveso_iii_regime.legal_basis: Uredba o preprečevanju in zmanjšanju posledic večjih nesreč (Uradni list RS, št. 22/16 in 68/19), Aneks 1 — dangerous substance thresholds.
seveso_iii_regime.notes: ARSO is the primary SEVESO III competent authority in Slovenia. Upper-tier sites may not operate until ARSO has assessed the varnostno poročilo. Pre-application engagement with ARSO is strongly recommended. Slovenian SEVESO practice is less developed than western EU peers — allow additional time for novel technology assessment. Proceedings in Slovenian language.

permitting.planning_authority: Municipality and Ministry of Infrastructure for spatial plan compliance; ARSO as key regulatory body for environmental permits and SEVESO III
parallel_consent_tracks: SEVESO III notification / varnostno poročilo (ARSO) — 3–12 months post-submission
Fire safety permit (Uprava RS za zaščito in reševanje) — concurrent with building permit
permitting.notes: P50 of 27 months assumes a lower-tier SEVESO III site outside Natura 2000 areas. Upper-tier sites add 6–12 months. Slovenia's permitting for industrial facilities is more streamlined than for wind (which requires spatial plan amendments). ARSO is a professional agency with English-speaking capability for international investors.

electricity_connection.relevance: Liquefaction is highly electricity-intensive (~10–12 kWh per kg H2 liquefied). Grid connection from ELES (TSO) or Elektro DSOs required for liquefaction plant.

liquefaction_specific_energy_kwh_per_kg_.source: IEA, The Future of Hydrogen, 2019, p.52

electricity_connection.grid_operator: ELES d.o.o. (TSO) or regional Elektro DSO (Elektro Celje, Elektro Ljubljana, Elektro Maribor, Elektro Primorska, Elektro Gorenjska)
electricity_connection.green_credentials_note: Slovenia's grid is ~60–70% hydro — inherently low-carbon but does NOT automatically qualify as green H2 feedstock under EU Delegated Regulation 2023/1184 without meeting additionality and renewable hourly matching criteria. A dedicated renewable PPA remains the recommended approach for green H2 certification.
electricity_connection.electricity_price_risk: MEDIUM — Slovenian industrial electricity prices broadly track HUPX-SI market price; connected to Italian and Central European markets. Verify current prices at AGEN-RS (agencija-energija.si).
electricity_connection.renewable_ppa_strategy: Pairing with a Borzen-tender solar PPA from Prekmurje or hydro bilateral PPA provides best combination of green credentials and price stability.
electricity_connection.notes: Prekmurje solar grid queue (Elektro Maribor DSO) is growing — early engagement with DSO for industrial connection is advised. Italian interconnection provides cross-border balancing capability beneficial for variable RES-backed electrolysis.

support_mechanisms._rule_3a_note: Rule 3A: LH2 storage has no dedicated two-way CfD or one-way FiT in Slovenia. Revenue is via tolling contracts with no state commodity floor. Investment relief (naložbena olajšava) is a tax base deduction, not a revenue mechanism.
support_mechanisms.primary_mechanism_standalone: Tolling arrangement — infrastructure fee for liquefaction, storage, and dispensing; no commodity price floor from the state.

investment_relief.description: Investment relief (naložbena olajšava — ZDDPO-2 §55): 40% of qualifying LH2 CapEx deducted from taxable base in the investment year, with 5-year carry-forward for excess. Applies to qualifying machinery and equipment including cryogenic tanks, liquefaction plant, and dispensing infrastructure.
investment_relief.note: This is a base deduction (not a CIT credit). Benefit = 40% × qualifying CapEx × 19% CIT rate, arising in investment year vs. distribution over project life.

eu_cohesion_funds.applicable: EU ERDF/ESF cohesion funds via SPIRIT Slovenia (spiritslovenia.si) may provide CapEx grants for qualifying H2 industrial infrastructure. SRIP Energetika H2 cluster provides R&D co-funding.
eu_cohesion_funds.staleness_warning: Programme calls are time-limited. Verify at spiritslovenia.si and eu-skladi.si for current open calls.

merchant_route.description: Standalone LH2 storage may operate on a merchant basis, charging industrial off-takers (Revoz/Renault, KRKA, Kolektor Group), transport sector (H2 bus/HGV fleet operators), and potentially Italian cross-border buyers via the Italian interconnection.
merchant_route.credit_risk_note: Merchant tolling revenues are contract-dependent; no state backstop. Industrial offtakers (Revoz, KRKA) are solid credit counterparties.

tax._rule_3b_note: Rule 3B: 19% CIT. Investment relief (naložbena olajšava — ZDDPO-2 §55) provides 40% of qualifying CapEx as a deduction from taxable base — a timing benefit, not a permanent rate reduction.

investment_relief.qualifying_assets: Qualifying equipment and machinery including cryogenic storage tanks, liquefaction plant, BOG management systems, dispensing infrastructure
investment_relief._rule_3b_analysis: For a EUR 40m LH2 project with EUR 35m qualifying investment: 40% base deduction = EUR 14m. CIT saving in year 1 = EUR 14m × 19% = EUR 2.66m (vs. standard depreciation distributed over 15 years). At 7% WACC, NPV acceleration benefit ≈ EUR 1.2–1.5m. Moderate but material timing benefit.

tax.dividend_wht_note: 0% dividend WHT for qualifying EU parent companies (ZDDPO-2 §71 — EU Parent-Subsidiary Directive)

safety_standards.cryogenic_vessel_standard: SIST EN 1251 (Cryogenic vessels — static vacuum-insulated vessels — Slovenian adoption of EN 1251)
safety_standards.electrical_hazardous_area: ATEX zone classification per Slovenian implementation of Directive 2014/34/EU; DSEAR equivalent per Pravilnik o eksplozijsko ogroženih območjih (Rules on Explosive Atmospheres)

boil_off_gas_management.regulatory_basis: BOG management must be addressed in varnostno poročilo and okoljevarstveno dovoljenje — ARSO governs atmospheric emissions.
boil_off_gas_management.environmental_note: Vented hydrogen is not a GHG (GWP ≈ 0) but is a safety hazard. ARSO permit conditions may limit vent frequency.

financial_model_inputs._rule_3d_note: Rule 3D: indicative inputs for IRR, NPV, CapEx payback, and DSCR modelling. Project-specific engineering estimates required before financial commitment.
financial_model_inputs._rule_3c_note: Slovenia is EUR-denominated — no FX conversion required.

illustrative_medium_scale_hub.scale: 2 tonne/day liquefaction capacity; 50 tonne vacuum-insulated storage

liquefaction_capex_eur.source: IEA, The Future of Hydrogen, 2019; Hydrogen Council, Hydrogen Insights 2023

illustrative_total_project_capex_eur.notes: 20% BOP and civil works allowance. Excludes grid connection reinforcement.

opex_electricity.source: IEA, The Future of Hydrogen, 2019; AGEN-RS industrial price reference (verify at agencija-energija.si)
opex_electricity.staleness_warning: Slovenian industrial electricity prices track HUPX-SI market. Verify before use.

illustrative_medium_scale_hub.note: Illustrative only. Actual costs depend on technology vendor, site, electricity procurement strategy, and supply chain.

market_context.si_hydrogen_policy: Slovenian NECP 2023–2030 includes H2 component. H2 blending trials in gas grid (Plinarna Maribor, Plinovodi) at early stage. Italian interconnection creates potential export route for Slovenian green H2. GEN-I is active in H2 energy trading. Kolektor Group is developing H2 components for the automotive supply chain.

key_si_lh2_projects_indicative.project: Revoz (Renault) green factory H2 supply concept
key_si_lh2_projects_indicative.status: Conceptual — H2 supply for green manufacturing at Novo Mesto plant; verify current status at revoz.si
key_si_lh2_projects_indicative.note: Renault group has pan-European H2 ambitions; Novo Mesto plant is the Slovenian production base.

key_si_lh2_projects_indicative.status: Pilot stage — H2 bus operations; LH2 bunkering requirement
key_si_lh2_projects_indicative.note: H2 mobility creates local LH2 demand for refuelling infrastructure.

primary_demand_drivers: Automotive sector decarbonisation (Revoz/Renault — largest Slovenian manufacturer)
H2 mobility — bus and HGV fleet operators
Industrial export — green H2 to Italian market via 400kV interconnection route
market_context.italian_market_note: Slovenia's 400kV interconnection with Italy and its position in the Italian/Central European price zone creates access to Italian green H2 buyers and importers — a significantly larger market than the domestic Slovenian economy. This cross-border export potential is a genuine differentiator for Slovenian LH2 projects.

lh2_storage.notes: Slovenia's LH2 storage investment case benefits from the Golob government's pro-RES stance, an inherently low-carbon grid (~60–70% hydro), the SEVESO III regime administered by a professional ARSO, and genuine Italian market access via 400kV interconnection. The absence of a dedicated LH2 revenue mechanism means investors rely on tolling contracts, the investment relief tax base deduction (ZDDPO-2 §55), and EU cohesion funds. Electricity cost is the primary OpEx risk — a dedicated renewable PPA is required for green H2 certification under EU Delegated Regulation 2023/1184. ARSO's SEVESO III permitting pathway is demanding but structured. Italian cross-border export potential is a structural advantage over landlocked CEE peers. Verify all EU cohesion fund programme calls at spiritslovenia.si and eu-skladi.si before project commitment.
